title: Alt capital sustainability hinges on modeling, transparency, careful product sequencing: GC’s Rousseau

title: Recoletos Re 2026-1 cat bond underscores Mapfre Re’s disciplined risk management: CEO Rosa
title_original: Recoletos Re 2026-1 cat bond underscores Mapfre Re’s disciplined risk management: CEO Rosa
summary: Spanish global reinsurer Mapfre Re’s sponsorship of its third and largest catastrophe bond yet, the $200 million Recoletos Re DAC (Series 2026-1) issuance, underscores the company’s disciplined approach to risk management, as well as its commitment to securing well-aligned protection for Mapfre Group, Miguel Rosa, CEO of Mapfre Re has said. Miguel Rosa, CEO of Mapfre Re, commented: “This transaction underscores our disciplined approach to risk management and our continued commitment to securing robust, well‑aligned protection for the Mapfre Group. “By further integrating indemnity‑based catastrophe bond coverage into our reinsurance corporate program, we are supporting the Group’s resilience while making strategic and diversified use of capital markets capacity.” Mapfre Re also noted that through this Recoletos Re DAC (Series 2026-1) transaction, it continues to reinforce the use of alternative capital as a structural pillar of its retrocession and reinsurance purchasing strategy, which ultimately further enhances the Group’s resilience through diversified sources of capacity that complement traditional products.
why_matters: Nueva financiación que puede acelerar la competencia en el sector.

title: UCITS cat bond funds return 1.91% YTD to May 1st, 10.37% rolling 12-month performance
title_original: UCITS cat bond funds return 1.91% YTD to May 1st, 10.37% rolling 12-month performance
summary: UCITS catastrophe bond funds currently stand at an average return for 2026 through May 1st of 1.91%, while their performance over the last twelve months stands at an average return of 10.37%, according to the latest data from the Plenum CAT Bond UCITS Fund Indices . For the lower-risk UCITS cat bond funds the 12-month return now stands at 10.27%, while the higher-risk cat bond funds stand at an average return of 10.50%, with both those figures slightly up from the 12-month return a month ago. You can analyse the Plenum CAT Bond UCITS Fund Indices in our charts: Over the last month or so of data that is available, the lower-risk cohort of UCITS cat bond funds actually outperformed the higher-risk group of strategies.

title: Willis unveils sensor-based parametric flood insurance solution for UK racecourses
title_original: Willis unveils sensor-based parametric flood insurance solution for UK racecourses
summary: Willis, a business of insurance and reinsurance broker WTW, has launched a new sensor-based parametric insurance solution designed to protect UK racecourses against localised flooding. The launch of this sensor-based parametric solution comes as flood risk continues to rise across the UK, with related costs on track to rise by up to 50% and hit £3.6 billion by 2050. Royal Windsor Racecourse, one of 16 UK racecourses owned and operated by the policyholder Arena Racing Company (ARC), is the first of its locations to implement a sensor-based parametric flood policy.
why_matters: Nueva financiación que puede acelerar la competencia en el sector.
